prs.title: feat(frontend): add Drop Missing Values (DropNa) transformation UI
prs.body:
## Description

Adds the "Drop Missing Values" feature to the data transformation menu. The backend already supports the `dropNa` `operation_type`, so this connects perfectly to the existing `/transform` API route seamlessly matching exactly how `drop_duplicate` operates.

Changes included:
- Added `DROP_NA = "dropNa"` to `operationTypes.js`.
- Created `DropNaForm.jsx` matching the existing menu styling (Tailwind CSS, error handling hook, structure).
- Includes an "All columns" vs "Specific columns" mode toggle to properly populate the `columns: null` vs `columns: string` payload condition required by the backend.
- Appended `DropNaForm` to `MenuNavbar.jsx` with full state hook implementations for rendering and visibility.

Fixes #(issue number)

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## How Has This Been Tested?

The new form respects the established `transformProject()` API wrapper architecture. Form payload dynamically adjusts `drop_na_params` matching the exact schema definition validated in existing backend tests.

- [x] Existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests added
- [x] Manual testing

## Screenshots (if applicable)

N/A

prs.title: ci: add parallel backend and frontend CI workflow
prs.body:
## Description

Adds `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — a parallel CI workflow that runs backend tests and a frontend build + test on every push and pull request to `main`.

The repo already has `lint.yml` for code style checks; this workflow adds the missing test/build gate.

Fixes #(issue number)

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## How Has This Been Tested?

The workflow was validated locally against the project structure:
- Backend: `uv sync --frozen --extra dev && uv run pytest` — confirmed all tests pass with `DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./test.db`.
- Frontend: `npm ci && npm run build && npm run test` — confirmed `vitest run` script exists in `package.json`.

- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests added
- [x] Manual testing

## Workflow details

**`backend` job** (Python 3.12 / uv / pytest)
- Uses `astral-sh/setup-uv@v4` — consistent with the existing `lint.yml`.
- Caches `.venv` keyed on `uv.lock` hash for fast subsequent runs.
- `uv sync --frozen --extra dev` installs production + dev deps (pytest, httpx, ruff).
- `uv run pytest` with `DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./test.db` (required by `conftest.py`).

**`frontend` job** (Node 18 / npm / Vite / Vitest)
- `actions/setup-node@v4` with `cache: npm` keyed on `package-lock.json` — consistent with `lint.yml`.
- `npm ci` for reproducible installs.
- `npm run build` (Vite build).
- `npm run test` (`vitest run` — already defined in `package.json`).

Both jobs run in parallel. `concurrency` cancels stale runs on new pushes to the same ref.

## Screenshots (if applicable)

N/A

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review
- [x] I have added/updated documentation as needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] Tests pass locally

prs.title: fix(file_service): add CSV extension and 50MB size validation to store_upload()
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes two silent security and reliability gaps in `store_upload()` inside `app/services/file_service.py`:

1. **No file type validation** — any file extension was accepted and written to disk (e.g. `.exe`, `.zip`, `.xlsx`).
2. **No file size limit** — a multi-GB upload would silently consume all available disk space.

`validate_upload_file()` in `security.py` already enforces these at the HTTP layer, but `store_upload()` operates at the service layer and can be called directly, so it must enforce its own invariants.

Changes:
- Added `MAX_FILE_SIZE = 50 * 1024 * 1024` (50 MB) module-level constant.
- Validates file extension (case-insensitive `.csv` only) before any disk I/O; raises `ValueError("Only CSV files are supported. Got: {ext}")`.
- Reads file content to measure size; raises `ValueError("File size {n}MB exceeds maximum allowed size of 50MB")` if exceeded.
- Calls `file.file.seek(0)` after the size check so `shutil.copyfileobj` writes the complete file to disk.
- New test file `tests/test_file_service.py` with 11 tests covering all new validations.

Fixes #(issue number)

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## How Has This Been Tested?

All tests run with `pytest` against Python 3.12 using the project's own `pyproject.toml` dependencies. Disk I/O is mocked so tests are fast and hermetic.

- [x] Existing tests pass
- [x] New tests added
- [ ] Manual testing

**Test results:**
```
11 passed, 1 warning in 0.13s
```

New test classes:
- `TestStoreUploadExtension` (6 tests) — `.csv` and `.CSV` accepted; `.xlsx`, `.exe`, no-extension, `.zip` raise `ValueError` with the actual extension in the message.
- `TestStoreUploadSize` (4 tests) — under limit accepted, exactly at limit accepted, 1 byte over raises `ValueError` with actual MB in message.
- `TestStoreUploadPointerReset` (1 test) — verifies disk file bytes match original content byte-for-byte, proving `seek(0)` runs before the write.

## Screenshots (if applicable)

N/A

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review
- [x] I have added/updated documentation as needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] Tests pass locally

prs.title: test: add pytest tests for cast_data_type, trim_whitespace, drop_na, and apply_logged_transformation
prs.body:
## Summary

Adds three new pytest test classes to `dataloom-backend/tests/test_transformations.py` for transformation functions that had no test coverage.

---

## Changes

### `TestCastDataType` — 7 tests
| Test | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `test_cast_to_string` | Column values become string representations |
| `test_cast_to_integer` | Numeric coercion; non-numeric & `None` become `pd.NA` (`Int64`) |
| `test_cast_to_float` | String → float via `pd.to_numeric`; verifies `float64` dtype |
| `test_cast_to_boolean_truthy_falsy` | Handles `true/false/yes/no/1/0` |
| `test_cast_to_datetime` | Verified as `pd.Timestamp` |
| `test_cast_invalid_column` | `TransformationError` raised with `"not found"` |
| `test_cast_unsupported_type` | `TransformationError` raised with `"Unsupported target type"` |

### `TestTrimWhitespace` — 3 tests
| Test | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `test_trim_specific_column` | Trims named column; numeric columns untouched |
| `test_trim_all_string_columns` | `"All string columns"` sentinel trims every string/object column |
| `test_trim_column_not_found` | `TransformationError` raised with `"not found"` |

### `TestDropNa` — 4 tests
| Test | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `test_drop_na_all_columns` | `columns=None` drops rows with NaN in any column |
| `test_drop_na_specific_columns` | Only rows NaN in the named column(s) are dropped |
| `test_drop_na_empty_columns_list_raises` | `TransformationError` raised with `"must not be empty"` |
| `test_drop_na_nonexistent_column_raises` | `TransformationError` raised with `"not found"` |

---

## Test results

All **14 new tests pass** alongside the existing suite. No existing tests were modified.

```
14 passed, 1 warning in 0.10s
```

---

## Notes
- Follows the exact same class-based style, `sample_df` fixture, and `TransformationError` assertion patterns as the rest of the file.
- The `test_cast_to_string` test checks string values rather than dtype because pandas 2.x returns `StringDtype` (not `object`) for `astype(str)`.
- The `test_cast_to_float` test uses a DataFrame with decimal strings to guarantee `float64` output (integer-only input stays `int64` after `pd.to_numeric`).

prs.title: fix(adapter-pg): Fix PostgreSQL Bytes upsert regression in v7
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes critical regression in Prisma v7 where PostgreSQL upserts with Bytes primary keys fail on the second call.

## Problem
- Works in v6.12.0, broken in v7.0.1
- String ID upserts work fine, only Bytes IDs affected
- Error: "No record was found for an upsert"

## Root Cause
Inconsistent `Uint8Array` serialization between create and where operations in upsert.

## Solution
Added explicit `Uint8Array` handling in `mapArg()` to ensure consistent Buffer conversion.

## Testing
- [x] Reproduces the exact issue from bug report
- [x] Verifies fix resolves the problem
- [x] No impact on existing functionality

Closes #28824


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Ensures consistent handling of binary (Uint8Array) inputs for database upserts, preventing intermittent upsert failures.

* **Tests**
  * Added a regression test suite and test matrix with a PostgreSQL setup to validate bytes-based upserts, plus a test schema covering byte-array and string IDs.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: made some changes to fix deployment issue
Changes made for deployment
Academic Backend Integration
prs.body:
This PR implements full backend and frontend integration for the **Academic Information** section under user settings.

Users can now:

- ➕ Add their academic details (institute, branch, URN, year, semester, GPA, etc.)
- 💾 Store data securely in the backend (MongoDB)
- 🔄 Automatically fetch and display academic info when the user logs in
- ✏️ Edit and update their academic information via the UI
- 🔐 All routes are protected using JWT authentication

---

## 🛠 Backend Features

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /api/academic` | Fetch academic info for logged-in user |
| `POST /api/academic` | Create academic info (if not already present) |
| `PUT /api/academic` | Update existing academic info |
| `DELETE /api/academic` | Delete academic info |
| `POST /api/academic/upsert` | Create or update academic info in one call |

- Includes input validation using `express-validator`
- Uses Mongoose schema for data integrity
- URN is unique per user, with server-side conflict checks

---

## 🧪 Frontend Integration

- React form with fields mapped to the academic schema
- Data is fetched on login and displayed read-only by default
- Users can click "Edit Academic Information" to update
- Form is validated before submission
- Success/error messages shown to the user
- Form state syncs properly on save and cancel

---

## 📦 Dependencies

- `express-validator`
- `mongoose`
- JWT middleware for route protection

---

## 🧩 Additional Notes

- Admin route (`/api/academic/stats`) is scaffolded for future analytics
- This PR is scoped only to Academic data; Grades/Events handled separately

prs.title: fix: grade api integration
removed work page
✅ Implement Backend API for Grade Tracker
prs.body:
This PR implements the complete backend for the **Grade Tracker** feature. It supports CRUD operations for grade entries, enforces authentication, and ensures user-specific data access.

---

## 🚀 Features Added

- ✨ **CRUD Endpoints** for `/api/grades`:
  - `GET /api/grades` – Fetch grades for the logged-in user
  - `POST /api/grades` – Add a new grade
  - `PUT /api/grades/:id` – Edit an existing grade
  - `DELETE /api/grades/:id` – Delete a grade

- 🔐 **Authentication Middleware**:
  - Protects all routes using JWT
  - Ensures only authorized users can access their data

- 🧠 **User-Scoped Data**:
  - Grades are linked to `userId`
  - Prevents unauthorized access to other users' grades

- 💾 **MongoDB Model (`Grade`)**:
  - Schema includes fields: `semester`, `date`, `courseName`, `assignmentTitle`, `marks`, `maxMarks`, `expectedGrade`, `finalGrade`, `credits`, `userId`, timestamps

---

## 🧪 Testing Done

- ✅ Manual testing with frontend integration
- ✅ Verified token protection on all routes
- ✅ Confirmed database write/read for individual users
- ✅ Checked for validation and error handling

---

## 📚 Notes

- The frontend now dynamically fetches grades after login.
- Token must be included in `Authorization` header: `Bearer <token>`
- Validation and error messages follow consistent structure

prs.title: Implement Email Verification for User Registration
prs.body:
### Summary

This pull request introduces a complete email verification flow for new user registrations. Accounts are created in an unverified state and must be activated by clicking a unique link sent to the user's email. This enhances security and ensures the validity of user emails.

---

### Implementation Details

-   **User Model (`User.js`):**
    -   Added `isVerified` (boolean), `verificationToken` (String), and `verificationTokenExpires` (Date) fields to the schema.
    -   Added a `generateVerificationToken()` method to create and hash the token.

-   **Backend (`authController.js`, `authRoutes.js`):**
    -   The `/signup` endpoint now triggers an email with a verification link.
    -   A new `POST /api/auth/verify-email` endpoint handles token validation and account activation.
    -   The `/login` endpoint now blocks unverified users.

-   **Frontend (`EmailVerified.jsx`):**
    -   A new dynamic page at `/email-verified/:token` captures the token from the URL.
    -   It sends the token to the backend for verification and displays a success or error message to the user.

---

### How to Test

1.  Navigate to the `/signup` page and register a new user.
2.  Attempt to log in with the new credentials; it should fail.
3.  Check the email inbox (or Mailtrap) for the verification email and click the link.
4.  The verification page should display a success message.
5.  Log in again; it should now succeed.

---

### Demo Video

A walkthrough of the complete registration and verification flow. Click the thumbnail below to watch the video on Loom.

[[Loom Video Walkthrough](https://cdn.loom.com/sessions/thumbnails/7e36f1b52f054cf186d6dda31eef7a66-with-play.gif)](https://www.loom.com/share/7e36f1b52f054cf186d6dda31eef7a66?sid=8e644803-a07c-4803-b688-67f4d90c04d3)

prs.body:
## ✨ Feature: User Login Authentication

This PR introduces secure login functionality to the project using JSON Web Tokens (JWT). Users can now log in using their registered email and password, and receive a JWT token for authenticated access to protected routes.

### 🔐 What's Implemented
- `POST /api/auth/login` endpoint for authenticating users
- Password validation using `bcryptjs`
- JWT token generation with `jsonwebtoken`
- Error handling for:
  - User not found
  - Invalid password
- Frontend integration with the login form (`Signin.jsx`)
- Token stored in localStorage (ready for protected route usage)
- Redirect to dashboard on successful login
- Toast notifications for success and error states

---

- [x] JWT token returned on successful login

issues.title: 🎓 Academic Information Backend & UI Integration
issues.body:
## Description

We need to implement a backend service to handle **Academic Information** for users. This includes storing academic data (institute, branch, year, GPA, etc.), fetching it when a user logs in, and allowing the user to edit/update their information via the frontend UI.

---

## ✅ Requirements

- [x] Create `Academic` model using Mongoose
- [x] Build REST API endpoints:
  - `GET /api/academic` — Fetch academic info
  - `POST /api/academic` — Create academic info
  - `PUT /api/academic` — Update academic info
  - `DELETE /api/academic` — Delete academic info
  - `POST /api/academic/upsert` — Create or update in one call
- [x] Add validation using `express-validator`
- [x] Ensure `URN` is unique per user
- [x] Protect all routes with JWT authentication
- [x] Create corresponding frontend UI with editable form
- [x] Display academic info on user login
- [x] Allow editing and saving changes to academic info

---

## 🧠 Why This Is Needed

- To allow users to manage and view their academic profile in the settings page
- To support future features like grade tracking and graduation projections
- To keep user data synced between frontend and backend

---

## 🧩 Additional Notes

- Admin stats route (`/api/academic/stats`) is optional for future analytics
- This is a foundational step for academic and performance-related features

issues.title: 📌 Feature: Create Backend API for Grade Tracker Page
issues.body:
We need to build a backend API to support the **Grade Tracker** frontend. This backend will handle CRUD operations for grade entries and associate them with individual authenticated users. The data should be persisted in a database and fetched automatically when a user logs in.

---

## ✅ Requirements

### 1. Authentication
- Users must be signed in to access their academic data.
- Use JWT-based or session-based authentication.
- Protect all API routes using auth middleware.

### 2. CRUD API Endpoints
| Method | Endpoint            | Description                   |
|--------|---------------------|-------------------------------|
| GET    | `/api/grades`       | Fetch all grades for the user |
| POST   | `/api/grades`       | Add a new grade               |
| PUT    | `/api/grades/:id`   | Update a specific grade       |
| DELETE | `/api/grades/:id`   | Delete a specific grade       |

### 3. Database Schema
Each grade should contain the following fields:

- `userId`: ID of the authenticated user (foreign key)
- `semester`: string (e.g., "Sem 5")
- `date`: date string
- `courseName`: string
- `assignmentTitle`: string
- `marks`: number
- `maxMarks`: number
- `expectedGrade`: string (optional)
- `finalGrade`: string
- `credits`: number
- `createdAt`: timestamp
- `updatedAt`: timestamp

> ⚠️ Ensure all grades are filtered by `userId` when fetching.

### 4. Authorization
- Only allow users to read/write their own grades.
- Prevent access to other users' data.

---

## 🛠️ Suggested Tech Stack

- **Database**: MongoDB (Mongoose) or PostgreSQL (Prisma)
- **Server Framework**: Express.js or Fastify
- **Authentication**: JWT, Passport.js, or NextAuth (if using Next.js)
- **Validation**: Zod / Joi / express-validator

---

## 💾 Example Grade Object (MongoDB)

```json
{
  "_id": "123456789",
  "userId": "user_abc",
  "semester": "Sem 3",
  "date": "2025-07-10",
  "courseName": "Data Structures",
  "assignmentTitle": "Project 1",
  "marks": 92,
  "maxMarks": 100,
  "expectedGrade": "A",
  "finalGrade": "A+",
  "credits": 4,
  "createdAt": "2025-07-11T10:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-07-11T10:00:00.000Z"
}

issues.title: Fix Black Bar at Bottom of Home Page
issues.body: There is an unwanted black bar visible at the bottom of the home page layout. It breaks the visual consistency of the UI and affects the overall design polish. #### 📸 Screenshot Reference See the bottom section in the attached screenshot — a dark black bar appears below the main content area. #### ✅ Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Remove or fix the black bar at the bottom of the home page - [ ] Ensure the background blends seamlessly with the rest of the UI - [ ] No extra scroll area or visual gaps should be present - [ ] Works correctly across different screen sizes (desktop, tablet) #### 💡 Possible Causes - Extra margin or padding on a parent/container element - Background color or height overflow issue - Improper use of `vh` or flex layout boundaries #### 📋 Additional Notes - Test with different viewport sizes - Make sure dark mode styles are not affecting the layout unintentionally

issues.title: Make Search Bar in Navbar Functional
issues.body:
The search bar in the navbar is currently non-functional. It needs to be implemented so users can search for content effectively.

#### ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can type in the search bar
- [ ] Search is triggered on Enter key press or search icon click
- [ ] Results are filtered or fetched based on the query
- [ ] Show relevant results or a “no results found” message
- [ ] Handle empty input without breaking

#### 💡 Suggested Implementation
- Add `onChange` and `onSubmit` handlers to the search input
- Create a function to handle search logic (e.g., filter local data or call an API)
- Use state to store and render search results
- Optional: Add a loading indicator or debounce search input

#### 📋 Additional Notes
- Ensure the feature is responsive and accessible
- Test with different input scenarios (e.g., long strings, special characters)

issues.title: Simplify Authentication – Use Only Email & Password Login
issues.body:
## 🛠️ Simplify Authentication – Use Only Email & Password Login

### 📋 Description
As discussed with the team, we’ve decided to **remove third-party login options** (Google, Facebook, Apple, Mobile) to reduce code complexity and save development time.

The main focus of the project is to **track user progress**, so simplifying the login system will help us stay on track and complete the core functionality on time.

---

### ✅ Tasks
- [ ] Remove Google login code/config
- [ ] Remove Facebook login code/config
- [ ] Remove Apple login code/config
- [ ] Remove Mobile number login (OTP-based)
- [ ] Ensure Email & Password login works correctly (signup, login, auth flow)
- [ ] Clean up related UI components
- [ ] Update any related documentation

---

### 🚀 Priority
**High**

### 🏷️ Labels
`enhancement` `auth` `cleanup`

---

### 📝 Notes
We can revisit adding social/mobile logins later after the MVP is complete.
